title: Add a within-lap ranking model over drivers in the same race/lap
summary: Pit calls are partly relative, not purely absolute: strategists react to rivals, track position, and the limited set of realistic stoppers on a lap.
rationale: Pit calls are partly relative, not purely absolute: strategists react to rivals, track position, and the limited set of realistic stoppers on a lap. Ranking within each `Race_Year x LapNumber` query can model who is most likely to pit next even if binary probabilities are mis-scaled.
implementation_hint: Build query groups as all drivers on the same `Race_Year x LapNumber`. Train an `LGBMRanker` with `lambdarank` or `rank_xendcg` using `PitNextLap` as integer relevance, skipping all-zero queries if needed. Blend the ranker score with the existing binary classifier rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Captures competitive context and field-relative urgency that current row-wise features likely miss, improving ordering on difficult laps with multiple plausible pit candidates.
risk: Ranking optimizes NDCG-style query ordering, not ROC AUC directly. Many laps may have zero positives, and careless query construction can make the task noisy.
